mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid drop in the unemployment rate below 4.5 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous real GDP growth above 2.5 percent and core inflation below 2.5 percent indicates a stable demand environment that supports corporate profit margins without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, yielding positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 60% of rolling 60-day windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy business investment, which directly lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, indicating that supply expansion has peaked and a demand contraction is imminent.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which drags down aggregate demand and produces negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if housing starts fall by more than 15% but the S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day forward returns in over 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates a soft landing where labor demand supports earnings without forcing restrictive monetary policy, generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when payroll growth exceeds 200,000 but the Fed raises rates, resulting in negative forward returns in more than 50% of such episodes over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in revolving consumer credit reflects sustained household borrowing capacity and consumption demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month period.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of instances where RSAFS rises for three consecutive months, indicating that credit growth has decoupled from real economic activity.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.4% threshold but the S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, indicating that markets price in eventual disinflation.
